passages.text: Neuroplasticity, also known as brain plasticity, is the ability of the brain to change throughout an individual's life. This process involves the strengthening or weakening of neural connections, as well as the growth of new neurons. For a long time, it was believed that the brain's structure was relatively fixed after a certain age. However, research has shown that the brain is highly adaptable and can reorganize itself in response to learning, experience, and even injury. For instance, when one part of the brain is damaged, other parts can sometimes take over its functions. This adaptability is crucial for cognitive development and recovery from neurological conditions.

The Hellenistic period covers the period of Mediterranean history between the death of Alexander the Great in 323 BC and the emergence of the Roman Empire. During this time, Greek cultural influence and language reached their zenith in the Mediterranean and Near East. This era was characterized by a new wave of Greek colonization which established Greek cities and kingdoms in Asia and Africa. This resulted in the export of Greek language and culture to these new domains, but also saw the Greek settlers influenced by local traditions. The period was also marked by significant advancements in various fields, including philosophy, science, and art, creating a unique synthesis of Eastern and Western thought.

passages.title: Cloze Test 17-21
passages.text: The discovery of the new antibiotic was so significant that it changed the course of medical history.

passages.title: Cloze Test 22-26

Interviewer: How do you handle high-pressure situations in a fast-paced work environment? 
Candidate: --- 
Interviewer: That's a good strategy. Prioritizing tasks effectively is key to maintaining productivity.

questions.question: Many experts believe that the integration of artificial intelligence into healthcare will revolutionize diagnostic accuracy and patient outcomes.

options.A: Pek çok uzman, yapay zekanın sağlık hizmetlerine entegrasyonunun teşhis doğruluğunda ve hasta sonuçlarında devrim yaratacağına inanmaktadır.
options.B: Birçok uzmana göre yapay zeka ve sağlık hizmetleri birleşince teşhis yöntemleri ve hasta sonuçları tamamen değişecektir.
options.C: Sağlık alanında yapay zeka kullanımının artmasıyla teşhislerin doğruluğunun ve hasta sonuçlarının gelişeceğine inanan uzmanlar vardır.
options.D: Uzmanlar, yapay zekanın sağlık sistemine dahil edilmesinin doğruluğu artıracağını ve hastaları olumlu etkileyeceğini düşünmektedir.
options.E: Yapay zekanın sağlıkta kullanılmasının teşhis ve tedavi süreçlerinde devrim yaratacağı pek çok uzman tarafından onaylanmıştır.

questions.question: Despite being criticized for its high costs, the space exploration program has provided invaluable data about the origin of our solar system.

options.A: Uzay keşif programı yüksek maliyetleri nedeniyle eleştirilse de güneş sistemimizin kökeni hakkında paha biçilemez veriler sağlamıştır.
options.B: Yüksek maliyetleri yüzünden eleştirilen uzay programı, güneş sisteminin nasıl oluştuğuyla ilgili veriler sunmaktadır.
options.C: Eleştirilere rağmen uzay keşif programı maliyetli bulunmuş ancak güneş sistemi hakkında değerli bilgiler vermiştir.
options.D: Güneş sistemimizin başlangıcı hakkında paha biçilmez bilgiler sunan uzay programı, maliyetleri nedeniyle eleştiri almıştır.
options.E: Maliyeti yüksek olduğu için eleştirilen uzay keşif programı, güneş sisteminin kökenine dair önemli verileri toplamayı başarmıştır.

questions.question: Modern eğitim sistemleri, öğrencileri sadece akademik bilgiyle donatmak yerine onların eleştirel düşünme becerilerini geliştirmeyi amaçlamalıdır.

options.A: Modern education systems should aim to develop students' critical thinking skills instead of just equipping them with academic knowledge.
options.B: Education systems today focus on providing academic knowledge rather than improving the critical thinking skills of students.
options.C: Instead of academic knowledge, modern education systems prefer to teach students how to think critically.
options.D: The goal of modern education systems is to equip students with critical thinking skills as well as academic knowledge.
options.E: It is necessary for modern education systems to provide students with both academic knowledge and critical thinking skills.

questions.question: Küresel ısınma ile mücadele etmek için uluslararası toplumun karbon emisyonlarını azaltma konusunda somut adımlar atması gerekmektedir.

options.A: In order to combat global warming, the international community needs to take concrete steps toward reducing carbon emissions.
options.B: The international community is taking concrete steps to reduce carbon emissions to fight global warming.
options.C: To prevent global warming, carbon emissions must be reduced through international cooperation and concrete steps.
options.D: If the international community wants to stop global warming, they should reduce carbon emissions immediately.
options.E: Global warming can only be fought if international organizations take concrete steps regarding carbon emissions.

questions.question: Boşluk 17 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The new antibiotic was important enough to alter the path of medicine forever.
options.B: Medical history was changed by the discovery of an antibiotic that was relatively important.
options.C: The significance of the new antibiotic was underestimated until it changed medical history.
options.D: Changing medical history required the discovery of a very important antibiotic.
options.E: The antibiotic discovered was significant, but its impact on medical history is still debated.

questions.question: Boşluk 18 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The stock market started to show instability immediately after the government's announcement of the new tax policy.
options.B: The government announced the new tax policy because the stock market was fluctuating.
options.C: As soon as the stock market fluctuated, the government announced a new tax policy.
options.D: The stock market's fluctuation led the government to announce a new tax policy very quickly.
options.E: The government was about to announce the tax policy when the stock market fluctuated.

questions.question: Boşluk 19 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Rigorous experimentation is the only way to prove that a scientific theory is valid.
options.B: Scientific theories are usually valid, even without rigorous experimentation.
options.C: If a theory is scientific, it must have been established through some experimentation.
options.D: Establishing the validity of a theory is more important than rigorous experimentation.
options.E: A scientific theory can be valid only if it is supported by various types of experiments.

questions.question: Boşluk 20 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Critics found the architect's design creative but argued it was not practical for modern cities.
options.B: The architect's design was neither creative nor practical for a modern urban environment.
options.C: The lack of creativity in the architect's design made it impractical for modern urban settings.
options.D: In modern urban settings, the architect's design was criticized because it was too creative.
options.E: The design was criticized for being impractical, which led to a lack of creativity in the urban setting.

options.C: Could you suggest some authors that I might have missed?
options.D: I am planning to add that section in the final draft of my thesis.
options.E: The topic is so new that there isn't much literature available yet.

options.D: I ask my colleagues to help me with the work I cannot finish.

options.D: Do you feel stressed due to your work or personal life?

options.C: We should probably recheck the data for any potential errors.
options.D: The study was conducted during the hottest months of the year.
options.E: I believe the public is already aware of the climate change issue.

questions.question: Cognitive biases are systematic patterns of deviation from norm or rationality in judgment. They often result from the brain's attempt to simplify information processing. ---. While these shortcuts allow for quick decisions, they can lead to inaccurate conclusions and irrational behavior.

options.A: Heuristics are mental shortcuts that the brain uses to solve problems.
options.B: Most people believe they are immune to such psychological influences.
options.C: Scientists are studying the neural pathways associated with decision-making.
options.D: The concept was first introduced by Tversky and Kahneman in the 1970s.
options.E: Education can help individuals become more aware of their own biases.

questions.question: The concept of 'smart cities' involves the integration of information technology to improve the quality of urban services. By using sensors and data analytics, cities can manage resources like water and electricity more efficiently. ---. For instance, real-time data can be used to optimize traffic flow and reduce congestion.

options.A: Privacy concerns are often raised regarding the collection of personal data.
options.B: The infrastructure required for such technologies is extremely expensive.
options.C: This leads to a more sustainable and livable environment for residents.
options.D: Many people are moving from rural areas to cities in search of work.
options.E: Cybersecurity is a major challenge for the implementation of smart grids.

questions.question: It was not until the late 19th century that microbiology began to emerge as a distinct scientific discipline.

options.A: Microbiology did not become a separate field of science until the end of the 1800s.
options.B: In the late 19th century, microbiology was the most popular scientific discipline.
options.C: Microbiology had been a distinct discipline long before the 19th century ended.
options.D: The emergence of microbiology as a science happened suddenly in the early 1900s.
options.E: By the late 19th century, scientists had already established the foundations of microbiology.

options.B: The board of directors has already approved the budget for the successful project.
options.C: Unless the project is successful, the board will not approve the budget.
options.D: The project will be successful even if the board does not approve the budget.
options.E: The budget was approved by the board to ensure the success of the project.

questions.question: Hardly anyone expected the underdog team to win the championship against such strong opponents.

options.B: Everyone was certain that the underdog team would lose the championship.
options.C: Only a few people were surprised when the underdog team won the title.
options.D: The strong opponents expected the underdog team to win the championship.
options.E: The underdog team was expected to win by at least a few dedicated fans.

questions.question: (I) Quantum mechanics is a fundamental theory in physics that describes the physical properties of nature at the scale of atoms and subatomic particles. (II) It is the foundation of all quantum physics including quantum chemistry and quantum technology. (III) Albert Einstein was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics for his explanation of the photoelectric effect. (IV) Unlike classical physics, quantum mechanics deals with probabilities rather than certainties. (V) This makes it a highly counter-intuitive but extremely successful mathematical framework.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) The Great Wall of China is one of the most impressive architectural feats in human history. (II) It was built over several centuries by various dynasties to protect against invasions. (III) Most of the existing wall that we see today was constructed during the Ming Dynasty. (IV) China's economy has grown rapidly over the last few decades, making it a global superpower. (V) Although it is often said to be visible from space, this is actually a common misconception.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and some other organisms use sunlight to synthesize foods. (II) It generally involves the green pigment chlorophyll and generates oxygen as a byproduct. (III) Without this process, life as we know it would not exist on Earth. (IV) Many plants are adapted to live in desert environments where water is scarce. (V) It provides the primary energy source for nearly all food chains.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: According to the passage, the primary role of neuroplasticity is to ---.

options.A: allow the brain to change and adapt throughout an individual's life
options.B: ensure that all brain functions remain constant from birth
options.C: prevent the formation of new neural connections in adulthood
options.D: limit the brain's ability to recover from physical injuries
options.E: store memories only during the early stages of childhood

questions.question: The passage states that the traditional view of the brain's structure was that it ---.

options.A: was mostly unchangeable after a specific period of life
options.B: constantly grew new neurons regardless of age
options.C: could only be modified through surgical interventions
options.D: was solely responsible for physical movements
options.E: had no capacity for learning after childhood

questions.question: It can be inferred from the passage that if a person suffers a brain injury, ---.

options.A: the brain may be able to compensate by using other neural pathways
options.B: the damage is always permanent and cannot be mitigated
options.C: neural connections will stop forming throughout the brain
options.D: they will lose the ability to learn any new information
options.E: the brain's plasticity will immediately cease to function

questions.question: What is the main focus of the passage regarding the Hellenistic period?

options.A: The spread and blending of Greek culture with other traditions
options.B: The military strategies used by Alexander the Great
options.C: The fall of the Persian Empire and its consequences
options.D: The architectural styles of ancient Greek temples
options.E: The democratic systems of the Greek city-states

questions.question: According to the passage, the Hellenistic period began ---.

options.A: with the death of Alexander the Great
options.B: when the Roman Empire was at its peak
options.C: after the total destruction of Greek city-states
options.D: following the end of the Roman Republic
options.E: during the first wave of Greek colonization

questions.question: The passage suggests that the culture of the Hellenistic period was ---.

options.A: a mixture of Greek and local elements
options.B: purely Greek without any outside influence
options.C: primitive compared to earlier Greek eras
options.D: mainly focused on military expansion
options.E: hostile towards any form of scientific advancement
